2603.17228.title: From Drop-off to Recovery: A Mechanistic Analysis of Segmentation in MLLMs
2603.17228.paper_summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) are increasingly applied to pixel-level vision tasks, yet their intrinsic capacity for spatial understanding remains poorly understood. We investigate segmentation capacity through a layerwise linear probing evaluation across the entire MLLM pipeline: vision encoder, adapter, and LLM. We further conduct an intervention based attention knockout analysis to test whether cross-token attention progressively refines visual representations, and an evaluation of bidirectional attention among image tokens on spatial consistency. Our analysis reveals that the adapter introduces a segmentation representation drop-off, but LLM layers progressively recover through attention-mediated refinement, where correctly classified tokens steer misclassified neighbors toward the correct label. At early image token positions, this recovery is bounded by causal attention, which bidirectional attention among image tokens alleviates. These findings provide a mechanistic account of how MLLMs process visual information for segmentation, informing the design of future segmentation-capable models.
2603.17228.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）越來越多地應用於像素級視覺任務，但它們在空間理解方面的內在能力仍然不甚明瞭。<lb/>我們通過對整個MLLM管道進行逐層線性探測評估來研究分割能力：視覺編碼器、適配器和LLM。<lb/>我們進一步進行了一項干預基於注意力的敲除分析，以測試跨標記注意力是否逐步細化視覺表示，並評估圖像標記之間的雙向注意力對空間一致性的影響。<lb/>我們的分析顯示，適配器引入了分割表示的下降，但LLM層通過基於注意力的細化逐步恢復，其中正確分類的標記引導錯誤分類的鄰居朝向正確的標籤。<lb/>在早期的圖像標記位置，這種恢復受到因果注意力的限制，而圖像標記之間的雙向注意力則緩解了這一點。<lb/>這些發現提供了一個機制性解釋，說明了MLLMs如何處理視覺信息以進行分割，並為未來具備分割能力的模型設計提供了參考。
2603.17228.author: Boyong Wu et.al.
2603.17228.authors: Boyong Wu, Sanghwan Kim, Zeynep Akata
